name: Stored XSS
educational_objective: Understand how user-supplied content stored in a database can execute as JavaScript when rendered by a privileged user, without any crafted URL required.
scenario: ShopReview — a product review platform. The review text field accepts HTML and stores it directly to the database. When the admin opens the moderation panel, all reviews are rendered via innerHTML — executing any stored script payload.
ar_scenario: منصة ShopReview تخزّن نص المراجعة مباشرةً في قاعدة البيانات وتعرضه عبر innerHTML في لوحة الإشراف للأدمن.
prerequisites: Understanding of stored vs reflected injection

hints_metadata.content: The review you submit is stored and later displayed in the admin panel. Think about what gets rendered, not just what gets stored.
hints_metadata.ar_content: المراجعة التي تُرسلها تُخزَّن وتُعرض لاحقاً في لوحة الأدمن. فكّر فيما يُعرض وليس فقط ما يُخزَّن.

hints_metadata.content: Submit a review that contains an HTML tag with a JavaScript event handler. The review text is not sanitised before storage or render.
hints_metadata.ar_content: أرسل مراجعة تحتوي على وسم HTML مع معالج حدث JavaScript.

hints_metadata.content: Submit: as the review text, then click 'Trigger Admin Review'. The payload fires in the admin context.
hints_metadata.ar_content: أرسل كنص المراجعة، ثم اضغط 'Trigger Admin Review'.

flag_validation.note: Generated per lab session on /start, returned from adminReview endpoint on exploit

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: POST /reviews body={text:''} → stored in DB → POST /admin/review-trigger → admin panel innerHTML renders payload → fires
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Sanitise stored content at render time with DOMPurify; never trust DB data as safe HTML
